analysis.summary: Mastodon is a decentralized social networking server built on Ruby on Rails that implements ActivityPub protocol for federated communication. It provides REST and streaming APIs, supports multimedia posts, follows/followers relationships, and includes comprehensive moderation tools. The system uses PostgreSQL for persistence, Redis for caching and job queues, and a React.js frontend for the web interface.
capabilities: Implements ActivityPub federation protocol for cross-server communication (app/controllers/activitypub/)
Processes incoming ActivityPub messages and dispatches to appropriate handlers (app/controllers/activitypub/inboxes_controller.rb)
Manages user authentication with Devise including 2FA and WebAuthn (app/controllers/auth/)
Provides OAuth2 authorization server for third-party applications (app/controllers/oauth/)
Handles media file uploads, processing, and storage with multiple backends (app/models/media_attachment.rb)
Implements real-time streaming API using Node.js and WebSockets (streaming/)
Manages follow/unfollow relationships and social graph (app/models/follow.rb)
Processes status updates including text, media, polls, and quotes (app/models/status.rb)
Implements content filtering and moderation with custom rules (app/models/filter.rb)
Handles notifications with grouping and filtering policies (app/models/notification.rb)
Provides full-text search using Elasticsearch integration (app/chewy/)
Manages user timelines with home, public, and list feeds (app/controllers/api/v1/timelines/)
Implements content warnings, sensitive media flagging, and interaction policies (app/models/status.rb)
Handles instance-wide and per-user blocking/muting functionality (app/models/block.rb, app/models/mute.rb)
Processes background jobs using Sidekiq for email delivery and federation (app/workers/)
Manages custom emoji upload and federation across instances (app/models/custom_emoji.rb)
Implements trending hashtags and link detection algorithms (app/models/trending_tags.rb)
Handles user profile customization including bio, avatar, and header images (app/models/account.rb)
Provides comprehensive admin interface for instance management (app/controllers/admin/)
Implements rate limiting and spam detection mechanisms (lib/mastodon/rate_limiter.rb)
Manages scheduled status posting functionality (app/models/scheduled_status.rb)
Handles bookmark and favorite status interactions (app/models/favourite.rb, app/models/bookmark.rb)
Implements lists for organizing followed accounts into custom feeds (app/models/list.rb)
Processes domain blocks and allows for federation control (app/models/domain_block.rb)
Manages user invitations and registration approval workflows (app/models/invite.rb)
use_cases: Individuals use this to run their own social media instance with full control over data and moderation policies
Organizations deploy this to create internal social networks while maintaining federation with the broader network
Communities use this to build topic-specific social media servers with custom rules and moderation
Developers integrate with the REST API to build third-party Mastodon clients and tools
Instance administrators use the web interface and API to moderate content and manage users
analysis.target_audience: System administrators, Ruby developers, social media enthusiasts, privacy-focused users
api_endpoints: GET /api/v2/instance — Fetch server information and configuration (app/controllers/api/v1/instances_controller.rb)
GET /.well-known/nodeinfo — NodeInfo endpoint for federation discovery
auth_methods: OAuth2 with PKCE support (app/controllers/oauth/, Gemfile - doorkeeper)
Two-factor authentication with TOTP and WebAuthn (app/controllers/auth/, Gemfile - devise-two-factor)
HTTP Signature authentication for ActivityPub federation (app/controllers/concerns/signature_verification.rb)
integrations: PostgreSQL via ActiveRecord ORM (Gemfile - pg, config/database.yml implied)
Redis for caching, sessions, and job queues (Gemfile - hiredis, hiredis-client)
Elasticsearch for full-text search (app/chewy/, Gemfile - chewy)
AWS S3 for media storage (Gemfile - aws-sdk-s3)
Azure Blob Storage (Gemfile - jd-paperclip-azure)
OpenStack Swift storage (Gemfile - fog-openstack)
SMTP for email delivery (app/mailers/)
WebPush for push notifications (Gemfile - webpush)
Sentry for error monitoring (streaming/ Node.js service)
FFmpeg for media processing (Dockerfile, README.md requirements)
ImageMagick/VIPS for image processing (Gemfile - ruby-vips)

components: Main Rails application handling web UI and REST API (app/)
Node.js streaming service for real-time WebSocket connections (streaming/)
Sidekiq background job workers for async processing (app/workers/)
ActivityPub federation handlers for decentralized communication (app/controllers/activitypub/)
OAuth2 provider for third-party app authentication (app/controllers/oauth/)
architecture.data_flow: HTTP requests hit Rails controllers which interact with ActiveRecord models, background jobs are queued to Sidekiq via Redis, real-time updates flow through Node.js streaming service to WebSocket clients, ActivityPub messages are processed asynchronously and federated to remote instances
architecture.persistence: PostgreSQL primary database with ActiveRecord ORM, Redis for caching and job queues, Elasticsearch for search indexes, file storage on local disk or cloud providers (S3, Azure)
architecture.concurrency_model: Rails application uses multi-threaded Puma server, Sidekiq provides multi-threaded background job processing, Node.js streaming service uses event-loop concurrency
architecture.communication_patterns: REST API for client-server communication, WebSockets for real-time streaming, ActivityPub HTTP POST for server-to-server federation, Redis pub/sub for internal messaging
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Ruby gem-based extensions, middleware pipeline for request processing, Sidekiq middleware for job processing, webhook system for external integrations (app/controllers/admin/webhooks_controller.rb)

storage_backends: PostgreSQL for relational data and JSONB fields
Redis for caching, sessions, and job queues
File system or cloud storage for media assets
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational database with ActiveRecord associations, JSONB columns for semi-structured data (status metadata, account fields), full-text search indexes in Elasticsearch, polymorphic associations for notifications and reports
data_model.migration_strategy: Rails ActiveRecord migrations with versioned schema changes (db/migrate/), post-deployment migrations for large table changes (db/post_migrate/)

algorithms.problem_type: Distributed social media with content discovery, federation, and moderation
core_patterns: Observer pattern for activity propagation and notifications
Strategy pattern for different authentication methods
Factory pattern for ActivityPub object creation
Command pattern for background job processing
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Large codebase with 9651+ files, complex federation protocol implementation, real-time streaming requirements, multi-tenant moderation system
key_algorithms: Trending calculation based on engagement metrics (lib/mastodon/trending_tags.rb)
Rate limiting with sliding window algorithm (lib/mastodon/rate_limiter.rb)
algorithms.evidence: app/services/activitypub/ contains federation logic, app/workers/ contains background job implementations, app/models/ contains business logic and associations

security_model.authentication: Multi-factor authentication with TOTP, WebAuthn, session-based auth via Devise, OAuth2 for API access
security_model.authorization: Role-based permissions for admin functions, per-user privacy settings, instance-level moderation controls
security_model.data_protection: HTTPS enforcement, secure headers middleware, password hashing via Devise, encrypted credentials in Rails
security_model.security_headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options implemented via Rails security middleware
security_model.vulnerability_handling: SECURITY.md file present, dependency scanning via bundler-audit implied, regular security updates

infrastructure: ImageMagick or libvips for image processing

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker Compose provided, Kubernetes via separate chart repository
deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions (test workflows visible in README badges)
deployment.environment_config: Environment variables for configuration, Rails credentials for secrets, dotenv support in development
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling via multiple Rails instances, separate streaming service, Redis cluster support, CDN for media assets

code_quality_signals.code_style: RuboCop for Ruby, Stylelint for CSS, TypeScript for frontend type checking

project_health.recent_activity: Recent commit focuses on federation improvements with collection item handling
project_health.community_signals: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, issue templates, comprehensive documentation

information_gaps: Limited git history in sample (only 1 commit)
Cannot determine exact Elasticsearch version requirements
Specific production deployment patterns not fully visible

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: The system processes incoming ActivityPub federation messages asynchronously, maintains real-time streaming connections via WebSocket, and uses event-driven background job processing via Sidekiq. Core pattern is reactive message handling with fan-out distribution to subscribers.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: ActivityPub protocol compliance with structured JSON-LD messages, REST API with defined endpoints, but also supports JSONB flexible fields and polymorphic associations for extensibility
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from ActivityPub message parsing/validation, timeline fan-out algorithms, notification grouping, real-time streaming distribution, and background job orchestration patterns throughout app/services/ and app/workers/
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from federated architecture allowing network partitions, Redis-backed job queues, asynchronous ActivityPub message processing, and timeline generation that can tolerate temporary inconsistencies between nodes
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Maintains a distributed graph of entities with bidirectional relationships, processes incoming modification events from federated peers, and propagates changes to interested subscribers in real-time while supporting content filtering and moderation policies.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Federated event ingestion → validation/filtering → graph mutation → fan-out notification → real-time stream distribution, with bidirectional sync across autonomous network nodes
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The core federation protocol handling, real-time streaming infrastructure, and graph relationship management are highly reusable. The ActivityPub implementation could be abstracted to any federated communication protocol. The domain-specific elements (social media posts, follows) are relatively thin layers over generic graph operations and pub/sub messaging. Main complexity is in the specific ActivityPub protocol compliance and social media moderation features.

evidence_claims.claim: Implements federated message processing with ActivityPub protocol
Uses event-driven architecture with background job processing
evidence_claims.evidence: app/workers/ directory and Sidekiq integration in Gemfile for asynchronous processing
streaming/ Node.js service with WebSocket support for live updates
app/models/follow.rb and relationship management in API controllers
Federation architecture allows autonomous instances with periodic synchronization

cross_industry_applications: Enterprise collaboration platforms (Teams/Slack alternatives with federation)
IoT device networks (federated sensor data sharing between organizations)
Financial trading networks (order book sharing between exchanges)
Gaming networks (federated leaderboards/achievements across game servers)
Academic research networks (federated data sharing between institutions)
Healthcare networks (federated patient record sharing with privacy controls)
Content delivery networks (federated cache invalidation and content distribution)

friction_report.migration_path: Extract the federation protocol handlers, streaming infrastructure, and graph relationship models into a generic framework, replace ActivityPub-specific message formats with domain-appropriate schemas, adapt the real-time streaming for target use case

comparable_systems: Apache Kafka (distributed event streaming with topic-based fan-out)
Git forge federation (distributed code repository synchronization)
Email servers with SMTP (federated message routing between autonomous domains)
Blockchain networks (distributed consensus with peer-to-peer propagation)
information_gaps: Performance characteristics under high federation load
Database sharding/partitioning strategies for large instances
